cover_letter: January 15, 2023 The American Israel Public Affairs Committee 251 H St NW Washington, DC 20001 Dear Selection Committee, I am writing to express my interest in the AIPAC Diamond Summer Internship Program. Having recently attended the Political Student Leadership Summit, where I had the privilege of lobbying on Capitol Hill, I am eager to bring my communication skills and passion for political activism to AIPAC. My interest in political engagement derives from a belief in the ability of individuals to make a positive difference through advocacy. I am particularly drawn to AIPAC's commitment to strengthening the US-Israel relationship, and I am eager to contribute to the organization's mission. During the Political Student Leadership Summit, I strengthened my persuasive communication skills while advocating for critical legislation. Through engaging with elected officials with BUCKiPAC, I have improved my ability to grasp policy issues, engage with stakeholder groups, and manage the complexities of the political scene. In addition, during my year-long campus engagement internship with OSU Hillel, I developed creative strategies to engage effectively with new students across campus.
extra_context: Interested in finding something she will enjoy and grow from.
jobText: Policy Analyst - Human Resources Department. Under administrative direction, responsible for planning and administering a specific policy analysis in the Human Resources Department. Responsibilities include planning and administering projects or programs, supervising activities of personnel, monitoring budgets, designing and monitoring procedures utilized in program evaluation, developing commitments from departments and agencies, reviewing policies and procedures, preparing project status reports, preparing financial and narrative reports, communicating with municipal officials and public and private entities, and performing related duties. Essential functions include policy development, data analysis, compliance and research, advisement, and training. Qualifications include two years of equivalent education training and experience in program administration required, five or more years of related experience preferred, public sector experience preferred, SHRM or project management certification preferred, excellent communication, prioritization, organizational skills, attention to detail, time management, and Microsoft Office proficiency including SharePoint.
